input.task: Explain why the ML lifecycle is described as circular rather than linear.

content.answer_preview: The ML lifecycle is described as circular rather than linear because the process involves continuous monitoring and feedback, which leads to data collection being influenced by the results of the monitoring phase (Page 91, Chunk 91). This cyclical nature ensures that improvements can be made iteratively, leading to better models over time.

final_answer: The ML lifecycle is described as circular rather than linear because the process involves continuous monitoring and feedback, which leads to data collection being influenced by the results of the monitoring phase (Page 91, Chunk 91). This cyclical nature ensures that improvements can be made iteratively, leading to better models over time.
